children.text: it is the policy and responsibility of the Federal Government to reduce the rate at which and the degree to which families depend on income from welfare programs and the duration of welfare receipt, consistent with other essential national goals;

it is the policy of the United States to strengthen families, to ensure that children grow up in families that are economically self-sufficient and that the life prospects of children are improved, and to underscore the responsibility of parents to support their children;

the Federal Government should help welfare recipients as well as individuals at risk of welfare receipt to improve their education and job skills, to obtain child care and other necessary support services, and to take such other steps as may be necessary to assist them to become financially independent; and

it is the purpose of this section to provide the public with generally accepted measures of welfare receipt so that it can track such receipt over time and determine whether progress is being made in reducing the rate at which and, to the extent feasible, the degree to which, families depend on income from welfare programs and the duration of welfare receipt.

children.heading: Development of welfare indicators and predictors
children.label: b Development of welfare indicators and predictors
A
children.text: indicators of the rate at which and, to the extent feasible, the degree to which, families depend on income from welfare programs and the duration of welfare receipt; and

children.label: B
children.text: predictors of welfare receipt;

assess the data needed to report annually on the indicators and predictors, including the ability of existing data collection efforts to provide such data and any additional data collection needs; and

children.text: the Committee on Ways and Means of the House of Representatives;

children.heading: Advisory Board on Welfare Indicators
children.label: c Advisory Board on Welfare Indicators

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established an Advisory Board on Welfare Indicators (in this subsection referred to as the “Board”).

children.heading: Composition
children.label: 2 Composition
children.text: The Board shall be composed of 12 members with equal numbers to be appointed by the House of Representatives, the Senate, and the President. The Board shall be composed of experts in the fields of welfare research and welfare statistical methodology, representatives of State and local welfare agencies, and organizations concerned with welfare issues.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 3 Vacancies
children.text: Any vacancy occurring in the membership of the Board shall be filled in the same manner as the original appointment for the position being vacated. The vacancy shall not affect the power of the remaining members to execute the duties of the Board.

children.label: A
children.text: providing advice and recommendations to the Secretary on the development of indicators of the rate at which and, to the extent feasible, the degree to which, families depend on income from welfare programs and the duration of welfare receipt; and

children.label: B
children.text: providing advice on the development and presentation of annual reports required under subsection (d).

children.heading: Travel expenses
children.label: 5 Travel expenses
children.text: Members of the Board shall not be compensated, but shall receive travel expenses, including per diem in lieu of subsistence, at rates authorized for employees of agencies under subchapter I of chapter 57 of title 5 for each day the member is engaged in the performance of duties away from the home or regular place of business of the member.

children.heading: Detail of Federal employees
children.label: 6 Detail of Federal employees
children.text: The Secretary shall detail, without reimbursement, any of the personnel of the Department of Health and Human Services to the Board to assist the Board in carrying out its duties. Any detail shall not interrupt or otherwise affect the civil service status or privileges of the Federal employee.

children.heading: Voluntary service
children.label: 7 Voluntary service
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Board may accept the voluntary services provided by a member of the Board.section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: Termination of Board
children.label: 8 Termination of Board
children.text: The Board shall be terminated at such time as the Secretary determines the duties described in paragraph (4) have been completed, but in any case prior to the submission of the first report required under subsection (d).

children.heading: Preparation
children.label: 1 Preparation
children.text: The Secretary shall prepare annual reports on welfare receipt in the United States.

children.heading: Coverage
children.label: 2 Coverage
children.text: The report shall include analysis of families and individuals receiving assistance under means-tested benefit programs, including the program of aid to families with dependent children under part A of subchapter IV of this chapter, the supplemental nutrition assistance program under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ( et seq.), and the Supplemental Security Income program under subchapter XVI of this chapter, or as general assistance under programs administered by State and local governments.7 U.S.C. 2011

children.label: i
children.text: the rate at which and, to the extent feasible, the degree to which, families depend on income from welfare programs, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the duration of welfare receipt;

children.label: B
children.text: trends in indicators;

children.label: C
children.text: predictors of welfare receipt;

children.label: D
children.text: the causes of welfare receipt;

children.label: E
children.text: patterns of multiple program receipt;

children.label: F
children.text: such other information as the Secretary deems relevant; and

children.label: i
children.text: the rate at which and the degree to which families depend on income from welfare programs, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the duration of welfare receipt.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: 4 Submission
children.text: The Secretary shall submit such a report not later than 3 years after , and annually thereafter, to the committees specified in subsection (b)(3). Each such report shall be transmitted during the first 60 days of each regular session of Congress.October 31, 1994

children.heading: Short title
children.label: e Short title
children.text: This section may be cited as the “Welfare Indicators Act of 1994”.
